cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking for the main idea of the passage. The options are A to D. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand it properly. The passage starts by talking about London's subway system and its historical value, making it a must for visitors. Then it shifts to discussing how people on the subway don't make eye contact because they're busy. It explains that they're not reading books or having quiet moments anymore; instead, they use technology like smartphones. There's a mention of Apple products (iPhone, iPod) and various apps that commuters use to pass the time. The passage ends by saying even with all these distractions, people still remember to get off the train. Now, the main idea needs to encapsulate the overall focus. The first part about it being a must-visit is an important point, but the majority of the passage is about how technology has changed the way commuters spend their commuting time. So, even though option D is mentioned, the core of the passage is more about the technology and its impact on commuting habits. Let's look at the options again: A) London commuters are unfriendly to strangers – The passage says avoiding eye contact isn't rudeness but due to being busy, so this might be a distractor. B) Apple has earned a lot from 40k apps – This is a detail mentioned, but not the main idea. C) Technology is changing how London commuters spend travel time – This seems to cover the majority of the passage's content. D) Riding London subway is a must – This is
